private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Overland AI is a Series A-stage defense and security technology company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies across the Defense & Security sector—2 public peers and 8 private companies at Series B and adjacent stages, including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, implying $80M. Peer-median analysis examines the 8 same-sector comparable companies, which exhibit a median valuation of $1.0B with a range from $125M to $3.0B, directly implying $1.0B as a reference point.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $68M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $540M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Overland AI's revenue scale, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to the peer set. The peer median of $1.0B likely overweights later-stage, higher-revenue comparables; stage-progression anchors a more conservative floor. A Series A valuation in the $200M–$400M range would position the company at a meaningful discount to mature peer valuations while reflecting appropriate stage risk.
